If I do a free-standing insert, I have up to two pages with a free-standing insert instead of, you know, 200 words to try to sell that. So I prefer to put those on free-standing inserts if we're going to do that. But, you know, that's how we do it, and that's how we recommend for our clients. Very few people do we do it differently. What a lot of people want to do is they want to put coupons in there. For most businesses, that will actually hurt the readership of the newsletter, which in turn will hurt the response rate. And then like the worst, I shouldn't say the worst. One thing I see that is a mistake is that then they put coupons, so they have the address section on the top of page four, and on the back of page four is coupons. And so now people think it's an advertisement. And people read the newsletter differently when they fill it's an advertisement. It'd be like GKIC putting, you know, like it would be like GKIC increasing the number of pages by six in the newsletter and then putting a bunch of ads. But by the way, if you guys decide to do that, I'll buy one, but no, I'm just kidding. No, I mean it's not good. It's not good for the newsletter. It's not good for the quality. But there's nothing wrong with you guys flipping an insert saying, hey, you should go to Info Summit. Yeah, well that's exactly right. And what everyone needs to do is open your mind to, well, what can I do with an offer? Because literally I can't think of a business where there can't be an offer. A restaurant could have a special promotion, a special night, just for the newsletter readers. I just, the other day, went to a new Indian restaurant around the corner from my house. I mentioned Sean, you know, that they needed to do a newsletter. It's the exact thing and have a, you know, a members only night. So you could put that in there. My fiance, Karen, just recently went to a, it's like a cosmetic place where they had this new laser treatment and they had like a little seminar and all of these wealthy women came to the seminar and then bought like, you know, these laser treatment things were thousands of dollars. Again, so you've got to open up your mind to, hey, what can I do as a promotion this month that I can use a freestanding insert for? So what you don't want to say is my business is different and that this won't work because it absolutely, it absolutely will. So let's, what are some interesting ways that you have found that your clients are using a newsletter to get new business? So probably one of the like, kind of newer, more interesting ways that I've seen recently that we've been testing is taking that freestanding insert and instead of using it as a freestanding insert with like a piece of paper, sending a CD in the mail with the newsletter. And so, yeah, that's been interesting because you can get, you get a couple things, you get real estate in the car usually. If they take it and pop it in the car, which is where most people have CD players or they obviously can rip it to their phone or iPod or whatever. But that's been cool and kind of new and you can do it both as content if it's going to clients where you're just trying to provide extra value or you can do it in a sales format. So we do it as content but we're also testing it in a sales format where we kind of like, you know, this, someone interviews me, you know, but it's instead of this where it's you asking questions, we obviously script the whole thing. But, and then sell, you know, that way. And so that's a pretty interesting new way that I've seen people using it. So, variable data offers. So, so again, we talked about that a little bit with the free standing inserts but I'll give an example where, just to show people what's possible, I guess, you know, that kind of a completely different type of business. So if you've been around GKSC for a while, you may have heard the name Steve Adams. He owns Pet Supplies Plus and he's in Dan's Titanium Group. And so we've been doing a newsletter for his retail pet stores for quite some time now, maybe even two years and it has changed over time. And, you know, initially a retail pet store would not be something where you'd be like, yes, a newsletter, that's completely intuitive, we should do that. And so you do have to do some stuff different for his store but we have been able to do variable data offers. So on their, the month of their anniversary of being, you know, a client of the pet store, we, you know, they have a special offer that they get and only those people get it. You know, in their birthday month, I believe there's a different offer and so on and so forth. So that's been pretty, pretty interesting. Here's, so here's one way that's not so much about the newsletter but maybe it's just a whole strategy.
